data.title: Petco Stock Surges On Better-than-Expected Q4 Sales
data.description:
Petco Health and Wellness Co Inc (NASDAQ:WOOF) shares are trading higher Thursday after the pet retailer posted fourth‑quarter results that included a slight revenue beat.

  • Petco Health and Wellness shares are climbing with conviction. Why are WOOF shares rallying?

Revenue Beat Offsets Earnings Miss

Petco reported a quarterly loss of 1 cent per share, falling short of analysts' expectations for a 2 cent profit. Revenue, however, came in at $1.515 billion, just above the $1.513 billion consensus estimate.

Company Says Turnaround Work Is Done, Growth Phase Begins

"In fiscal 2025, we strengthened our leadership team and rebuilt the foundation of our economic model, enabling us to exceed our profitability goals," CEO Joel Anderson said.

"With that work largely complete, we are entering the next phase of our strategy focused on driving sustainable, profitable top‑line growth," Anderson added. That shift from defense to offense is typically welcomed by investors, especially in a retail environment where many companies are still struggling to regain momentum.

Petco expects fiscal 2026 net sales to be flat to up 1.5% year-over-year.

Petco Technical Analysis

Petco is trading 30.1% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 10.4% above its 100-day SMA, showing a sharp short-term snapback that's starting to repair the intermediate trend. Shares are up 31.71% over the past 12 months, and the stock is positioned closer to its 52-week lows than highs after printing a 52-week low on 2026-03-09.

The RSI is at 40.39, which sits in neutral territory and suggests the rally hasn't pushed momentum into stretched levels yet. Meanwhile, MACD is at -0.0839 and remains below its signal line at -0.0793, keeping a bearish tilt in place even as price lifts.

With RSI below 50 and MACD still bearish, the indicators point to mixed momentum — price is bouncing, but trend confirmation is still catching up.

  • Key Resistance: $3.50
  • Key Support: $3.00

Sector Performance: Petco is outperforming its Consumer Discretionary peers by about 33.90 percentage points today, a standout move considering the sector is down 1.61%. That relative strength matters because Consumer Discretionary ranks 10 out of 11 sectors (one of the weakest groups on the day), so buyers are clearly targeting Petco specifically rather than the group.

Zooming out, the sector has been under pressure, down 5.05% over the past 30 days and down 6.91% over the past 90 days. In that context, Petco's surge looks more like a counter-trend rally inside a still-challenged sector backdrop, making follow-through above resistance the key tell.

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold rating with an average price target of $3.73. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Jefferies: Upgraded to Buy (Raises Target to $5.00) (Mar. 12)
  • Evercore ISI Group: In-Line (Lowers Target to $3.00) (Feb. 3)
  • Evercore ISI Group: In-Line (Raises Target to $4.00) (Nov. 26, 2025)

WOOF Price Action: Petco Health shares were up 37.08% at $3.29 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Netflix Cuts Dozens Of Product Team Jobs Amid Internal Restructuring
data.description:
Netflix, Inc. (NASDAQ:NFLX) stock traded relatively flat on Thursday amid reports of workforce changes within its global product team.

• Netflix shares are consolidating. What should traders watch with NFLX?

The reported move reflects internal restructuring as the streaming company adjusts leadership responsibilities and team structures.

Benzinga has requested Netflix for their comments on the story and will update once we get a response.

Workforce Changes

Netflix recently eliminated several dozen positions within its global product team as part of an internal reorganization, Variety reports. The affected group primarily supported marketing design, promotional assets, and creative materials tied to content and product launches.

People familiar with the situation told Variety that the cuts targeted the company’s “creative studio unit”.

That team produces promotional materials such as posters, trailers inside the platform, and visuals used in live experiences.

However, the reductions did not stem from employee performance, Variety adds, referring to some sources with knowledge of the matter. Some workers lost positions, while others moved into different roles within the organization.

Financial Context

On March 6, Bank of America Securities analyst Jessica Reif Ehrlich maintained a Buy rating, lowering the price forecast from $149 to $125.

Earlier this month, Netflix said it acquired filmmaking technology company InterPositive, founded by American actor Ben Affleck.

In a release dated January 20, Netflix said it ended the fourth quarter with gross debt of $14.5 billion and cash and cash equivalents of $9 billion.

Leadership Realignment

The staffing changes follow a recent leadership transition inside the technology and product divisions. Elizabeth Stone recently advanced from chief technology officer to chief product and technology officer, Variety adds.

Her expanded responsibilities now cover product development, engineering, and data teams across the company. The restructuring aligns the product group with the broader technology organization.

While Netflix has not disclosed the exact number of affected workers, the reductions represent a small share of the overall workforce.

The streaming company employs roughly 16,000 people worldwide.

Most employees work in North America, while additional teams operate across Europe, the Middle East, Africa, Asia-Pacific, and Latin America.

NFLX Price Action: Netflix shares were down 0.01% at $94.88 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by DIA TV via Shutterstock

data.title: 5 Unloved Stocks Set to Take Off
data.description:
Despite fading consumer confidence, the restaurant industry appears positioned for its best performance in years. Valuations for restaurant stocks are approaching decade lows, which could make the sector an enticing value play if markets continue wobbling.

The sector also has multiple tailwinds in 2026, including larger tax returns spurred by last year's legislation and a host of global sporting events, such as the Winter Olympics, the World Baseball Classic, and (most importantly) the FIFA World Cup.

Elevated commodity prices and tariff pressures remain headwinds, but the valuations and growth potential of certain restaurants make them too enticing to ignore.

Today, we'll look at restaurant stocks with technical and fundamental tailwinds that could be industry leaders in 2026.

Here are the five restaurant stocks set to take off.

CAVA Group Inc.

CAVA reported earnings on February 24 and posted a top- and bottom-line beat, but the number that really stood out to Wall Street was 0.5% growth in same-store sales. Analysts had projected a loss of more than 1% in the category, so a positive quarterly number and 3-5% same-store sales guidance for 2026 triggered a wave of price target boosts.

CAVA shares cratered at the end of November, but the momentum swing since then has been nothing short of parabolic. The stock is up more than 35% already this year, and the earnings boost took the share price above the 200-day moving average for the first time since February 2025. Bullish momentum continues to swell thanks to a Golden Cross and a Relative Strength Index (RSI) that has receded from Overbought territory.

Brinker International Inc.

EAT doesn't report its next earnings figures until mid-May, but a few bullish technical trends have materialized on the daily chart. If a price channel is forming, shares are now bouncing off the lower trendline, and the RSI is skating at the Oversold threshold. The last time EAT shares dipped below 30 on the RSI, it triggered a parabolic rally over the next month, so keep an eye on the stock at this level.

Dutch Bros Inc.

Darden Restaurants Inc.

McDonald’s Corp.

Compare this to Wendy's same-store sales decline of more than 11%, and it’s not hard to see why MCD shares are up more than 6% so far in 2026. With operating margins over 40%, McDonald's can sell Value Menu items permanently without resorting to limited-time promotions.

The Q4 earnings release triggered a breakout from a consolidation zone around the 50-day and 200-day moving averages, and the stock is now trading firmly in a bullish pattern. The uptrend sent the RSI into Overbought territory, but now that the outsized momentum has peeled back, investors can reposition and wait for the next leg up.

data.title: Trader Flips $10K Into $53K With Netflix Calls — Lessons You Can Learn From the Trade
data.description:
A trader on Reddit's r/wallstreetbets says they turned roughly $10,000 into more than $53,000 on a Netflix Inc (NASDAQ:NFLX) options trade in just a few weeks, posting a screenshot that showed a gain of more than 424%.

The trade caught attention because it was tied to the market's shifting view of Netflix's pursuit of Warner Bros. Discovery and the possibility that Netflix could still come out ahead financially even if it lost the deal. 

The trader shared a position showing 100 Netflix call contracts with a $90 strike and a March 20, 2026 expiration. According to the screenshot, the contracts were purchased on Feb. 3 for an average cost of $1.01, or about $10,100 total.

By Feb. 27, those same contracts were marked at $5.30, putting the position's value at roughly $53,000 and implying a profit of about $42,900 in a little over three weeks—the kind of swing that attracts traders who want a place to both trade and earn 8.1% APY on idle cash while they wait for the next setup.

The setup drew praise from other users, with one commenter saying, "I love this trade. Bought on 2/3 with month plus to work out…either it would bounce [because] deal is off or bounce [because] deal going through. They got $2 [billion] in breakup fee so that's free money."

That comment needs context. The deal was Netflix's proposed acquisition of key Warner Bros. Discovery assets, and the breakup-fee thesis was real. 

Reuters reported last month that Paramount Skydance had offered to fund the $2.8 billion termination fee Warner would owe Netflix if Warner walked away from its existing agreement. In other words, even if Netflix lost the asset battle, investors could still view the outcome as financially favorable if Netflix avoided a massive acquisition and pocketed a multibillion-dollar fee.

That is effectively what happened. Later, Reuters reported that Paramount's revised offer was better, Netflix chose not to match it, and Paramount agreed to cover the $2.8 billion fee Warner owed Netflix. 

Netflix shares jumped more than 10% as investors cheered the company's decision to walk away rather than stretch further on price.

So the Reddit thesis was not that Netflix needed to win the deal at all costs. It was that either path could work for the stock. If Netflix completed the transaction, bulls could argue it was acquiring valuable studio and streaming assets. If the deal fell apart, Netflix could still emerge with a large termination payment and avoid taking on the added complexity and financing burden of a huge acquisition.

The trader also appeared to have a broader roadmap for the stock beyond the options trade.

"Once we break $100 and consolidate near there, I’ll probably enter a common position and hold," they wrote.

Sentiment across the thread was broadly bullish. Several users pointed to momentum in the stock and saw more room to run, especially if Netflix could clear the $100 level.

"To the moon. Netflix has at least another 10% upside in the next 2 months. At bare minimum," one commenter wrote.

The original poster echoed that view while acknowledging the broader market could still interfere.

"Yeah, reclaiming $100 seems likely if the market doesn't vomit too much. It might take until next earnings though," the trader said.

The trade itself is obviously an outlier. Most options bets do not turn $10,000 into more than $50,000 in a few weeks. But the structure behind it is what makes it worth studying.

There was a catalyst. There was a defined time frame. There was a profit-taking plan. And there was a specific market narrative behind the move, namely that Netflix was in a position where it could benefit either by landing Warner assets or by stepping aside and collecting a multibillion-dollar fee.

A 424% gain grabs the headline. The real lesson is that the trade was not framed as blind hope. It was framed around a scenario, and then managed with a plan. 

For traders who want to build their own version of that playbook, offers like moomoo's current promo, which pairs an elevated rate on swept cash with tiered Nvidia stock bonuses for eligible new deposits, are one way to make the waiting part of the process a little more productive.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Why Is MercadoLibre Stock Falling Thursday?
data.description:
MercadoLibre, Inc. (NASDAQ:MELI) shares fell Thursday after JPMorgan downgraded the Latin American e-commerce giant, warning that intensifying competition in Brazil and heavier investment spending could keep profit margins under pressure.

MercadoLibre Plans $3.4B Argentina Investment

Separately, MercadoLibre expects to invest $3.4 billion in Argentina in 2026, a roughly 30% increase from the $2.6 billion planned for 2025, CEO Ariel Szarfsztejn said, Reuters reported.

The investment will support logistics expansion, new distribution centers, technology upgrades, and growth of fintech unit Mercado Pago.

The company also plans to create nearly 2,000 jobs in Argentina, where it currently employs about 16,700 people, Reuters reported.

Technical Analysis

Mercado Libre is trading 11.3% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 19.6% below its 100-day SMA, keeping both the short- and intermediate-term trend pointed down. Shares are down 17.79% over the past 12 months and are now positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs after setting a new 52-week low on 2026-03-12.

The RSI is at 38.08, which sits in neutral territory but leans toward weakening momentum after the stock dipped into oversold levels on 2026-03-03. Meanwhile, MACD is at -79.2935 and below its signal line at -77.8404, reinforcing bearish pressure as downside momentum remains in control.

The combination of RSI in the 30–50 range and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $1810.50
  • Key Support: $1654.00

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 6, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: $10.67 (Up from $9.74 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $8.35 Billion (Up from $5.93 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 44.8x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $2748.75. Recent analyst moves include:

  • JP Morgan: Downgraded to Neutral (Lowers Target to $2100.00) (Mar. 12)
  • Morgan Stanley: Overweight (Lowers Target to $2600.00) (Mar. 9)
  • JP Morgan: Overweight (Lowers Target to $2650.00) (Mar. 2)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Mercado Libre, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Weak (Score: 12.53)
  • Quality: Weak (Score: 16.78)
  • Value: Neutral (Score: 39.58)
  • Growth: Strong (Score: 94.6)

The Verdict: Mercado Libre’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a “growth-first, trend-last” setup. Growth is the clear strength, but very weak Momentum and low Quality scores suggest traders may keep selling rallies until the stock can reclaim key moving averages and hold above resistance.

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because MELI carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

MELI Price Action: MercadoLibre shares were down 6.83% at $1645.45 at the time of publication on Thursday. The stock is trading at a new 52-week low, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock
